good_practice_name: Finding solutions for people displaced by the Hunga Tonga Hunga Ha'apai volcanic eruption and tsunami in Tonga
description: The Tongan government provided emergency assistance to people displaced by the Hunga Tonga Hunga Ha'apai volcanic eruption and subsequent tsunami on 15 January 2022, as well as longer-term solutions in the form of relocation to other islands and new homes, livelihood support, and considerations for social cohesion and psychosocial well-being.
image: Newly built homes for the relocated community from Atata island

good_practice_name: Down By The River project in Fiji
description: Oxfam's Down By The River project explores the extent to which sexual and gender minorities were included in DRR and humanitarian support to displaced people and vulnerable populations in Fiji after tropical cyclone Winston in 2016. The project used individual story-sharing, community mapping, and talanoa sessions.
image: Storm surge damage in Fiji. Credit: Danielle Parry, OCHA Pacific.

good_practice_name: Positioning Disaster Displacement in the Tongan Cluster System
implementing_entity: Government of Tonga and the Pacific Response to Disaster Displacement project
description: Tonga established a Displacement and Evacuation Management Centre Cluster (DEMCC) to enhance response mechanisms and reduce displacement risk across Tonga, led by NEMO and supported by IOM to coordinate roles and responsibilities across Tonga's cluster system.
image: Photo: Asian Development Bank - Hahake Coastal Protection

good_practice_name: Vanuatu's National Policy on Climate Change and Disaster-Induced Displacement
description: Vanuatu's National Policy on Climate Change and Disaster-induced Displacement is globally recognised for its comprehensive approach to addressing all aspects of the response to displacement. The policy includes an analysis of drivers, systems-level strategic priorities, and key actions to mainstream displacement into development.
image: Photo: Biodiversity International - Mother and baby pick leafy vegetables in the rural Efate island in Vanuatu

country: Fiji, Vanuatu, Kiribati, Marshall Islands, Nauru, French Polynesia (France), Solomon Islands, Tuvalu, Tonga, Papua New Guinea, Niue, Palau, Cook Islands, Samoa
good_practice_name: Framework for Resilient Development in the Pacific: An Integrated Approach to Address Climate Change and Disaster Risk Management (FRDP) 2017-2030
implementing_entity: Pacific Community (SPC), Secretariat of the Pacific Regional Environment Programme, Pacific Islands Forum Secretariat, United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction and University of the South Pacific
description: Provides high-level strategic guidance to enhance resilience to climate change and disasters in ways that contribute to and are embedded in sustainable development, focusing on integrated adaptation, low-carbon development, and strengthened disaster response.

good_practice_name: Fiji planned relocation guidelines - A framework to undertake climate change related relocation
description: Establishes overarching principles to guide planned relocation as an adaptation measure of last resort for communities highly vulnerable to climate change. Outlines three key stages: making the decision to relocate, planning, and implementation.
image: Global Environment Facility - PCCAPHH project - Wailea settlement, Fiji
